subject: Civics
chapter: Chapter: Parliament and the Making of Laws

key_points: Parliament is the central law-making institution in India that represents the will of the people
It consists of two houses: Lok Sabha (House of the People) and Rajya Sabha (Council of States)
Members of Lok Sabha are directly elected by citizens while Rajya Sabha members represent states
Parliament debates national issues and passes laws for the country
The government is responsible to Parliament and must answer questions raised by its members
Parliament controls the executive through discussions, debates and question hour
examples: Members of Parliament discuss public problems such as education, poverty and employment
During Question Hour, ministers must answer questions raised by Members of Parliament
A bill is introduced in Parliament and becomes a law after approval by both houses
Parliament debates policies before they are implemented
formulas: Indian Parliament → President + Lok Sabha + Rajya Sabha
Law making process → Bill → Discussion → Voting → Approval → Law
Democratic representation → Citizens → Elections → Members of Parliament
exam_tips: Mention the three parts of Parliament: President, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Explain the difference between Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha
Describe how Parliament controls the government
Write the steps involved in making a law
